title: StarCraft (video game)
content:
StarCraft is a 1998 military science fiction real-time strategy game developed and published by Blizzard Entertainment for Microsoft Windows. The game spawned the "StarCraft" franchise, and became the first game of the video game series. A Classic Mac OS version was released in 1999, and a Nintendo 64 adaptation, co-developed with Mass Media, was released in 2000. Blizzard started work on the game shortly after "", another real-time strategy game, was released in 1995.

The first incarnation debuted at the 1996 Electronic Entertainment Expo, where it was unfavorably compared to "Warcraft II". As a result, the project was entirely overhauled before being showcased to the public in early 1997, at which time it received a far more positive response. The game's multiplayer is particularly popular in South Korea, as of 2006, where players and teams participate in, earn sponsorships, and compete in televised tournaments.
